summary: Homepage performance slipped back to 67 (from 71) with LCP at 7.5 s — persistently well below 85 and unchanged in trend; /projects and both project detail pages regressed meaningfully (10–21 pts), while /about remains a bare placeholder with SEO below threshold.

pages.visual_fingerprint: Full-page portfolio homepage with teal-accented hero ('App' heading, 'Send me a message' CTA), four content sections (pillars, case studies, skills, contact form), and a dark contact footer — all sections visible and correctly laid out on both desktop and mobile.

visual_findings: Page contains only the placeholder text 'This is an about page' on an otherwise near-blank dark canvas — no real content, nav, or footer present on either desktop or mobile.
pages.visual_fingerprint: Nearly blank dark page with only the stub text 'This is an about page' in the upper-left corner and no other UI elements visible on desktop or mobile.

Fully styled projects index page with 'My Projects' heading, five project cards (Clinical Note Structurer, Screenshot → PrimeVue, Lease Diff Explainer, Paperwork → Calendar, Dependency Upgrade Planner) — desktop shows a 2-column grid with the fifth card in a single left-aligned row, and a dark contact footer visible on both desktop and mobile.

Clinical Note Structurer detail page with breadcrumb nav, 'Evals: 10/10 passing' badge, two-column visit note / structured result interactive demo, amber disclaimer banner, expandable JSON and HIPAA notes accordions, and a dark contact footer — correctly rendered on both desktop and mobile.

Screenshot → PrimeVue detail page with breadcrumb nav, 'Evals: 5/5 passing' badge, dashed-border image drop zone, three sample thumbnail cards (Login form, Analytics dashboard, Pricing page), a placeholder result panel, expandable accordions, and a dark contact footer — correctly rendered on both desktop and mobile.

top_fix: Fix the homepage LCP (currently 7.5 s, performance score 67, persistently below 85 across many consecutive audits): preload the hero image with `<link rel="preload" as="image">`, serve it in WebP/AVIF at mobile-appropriate dimensions, and defer render-blocking resources above the fold — this is the single highest-impact unresolved issue on the site.
issue_title: Homepage performance persistently below 85: score 67, LCP 7.5 s — no improvement trend across multiple audits
issue_body:
## Homepage performance remains critically slow

The homepage (`/`) scored **67** on today's mobile Lighthouse audit (2026-07-10), with LCP at **7,476 ms**. The score has oscillated between 67–71 across multiple consecutive audits with no meaningful improvement trend. This is a persistent regression that has been open for several audit cycles.

### Current metrics (mobile)
| Metric | Value | vs. Previous |
|---|---|---|
| Performance | 67 | -4 pts (within jitter, but persistently below 85) |
| LCP | 7,476 ms | -189 ms (effectively flat; critically slow) |
| CLS | 0.015 | stable |
| TBT | 99 ms | stable |

### Also regressed today
- `/projects` performance dropped from **86 → 76** (-10 pts, back below 85); LCP up from 3,414 → 4,275 ms (+861 ms).
- `/projects/note-structurer` performance dropped from **96 → 89** (-7 pts); LCP up from 2,099 → 2,904 ms.
- `/projects/screenshot-to-primevue` performance dropped from **97 → 89** (-8 pts); LCP up from 2,250 → 3,058 ms.
- The project detail page regressions may be transient (measurement noise), but the /projects page drop is consistent with the homepage pattern.

### Still open — /about
- `/about` remains an empty placeholder (stub text only, no nav/footer); SEO score 83, below the 85 threshold — unchanged across multiple audits.

### Suggested fixes for `/`
1. Preload the LCP hero image with `<link rel="preload" as="image">`.
2. Serve the hero in a next-gen format (WebP/AVIF) sized for mobile viewports.
3. Defer or remove render-blocking scripts/stylesheets above the fold.
4. Consider lazy-loading below-the-fold sections to reduce initial paint cost.

### Suggested fixes for `/projects` LCP regression
1. Ensure project card images (if any) are lazy-loaded below the fold.
2. Investigate whether a third-party script or new card (Dependency Upgrade Planner was added) is contributing to LCP delay.
